---
name: ci-devops
description: CI pipeline and build system for LinqStudio — Nuke Build.cs targets, GitHub Actions workflows, MAUI platform exclusion, and build commands. Use this when modifying .github/workflows, build/Build.cs, or diagnosing CI failures.
---

# CI / DevOps Skill — LinqStudio

## When to Use This Skill

Read this before:
- Editing or adding GitHub Actions workflows
- Changing build targets (Restore, Compile, Test, etc.)
- Debugging CI failures on ubuntu-latest or windows-latest
- Adding new projects that may have platform restrictions
- Investigating workload or restore errors in CI

---

## Source of Truth: `build/Build.cs`

**`build/Build.cs` is the single source of truth for the main CI pipeline.**

`build-and-test.yml` is **auto-generated** by the `[GitHubActions]` attribute in `Build.cs`. Do not edit it directly — changes will be overwritten the next time Nuke regenerates configuration.

To regenerate the YAML manually:
```
nuke --generate-configuration GitHubActions_build-and-test --host GitHubActions
```

The exception is `build-maui-windows.yml` — that file is **hand-authored** (no Nuke attribute) and safe to edit directly.

---

## Nuke Build System

### Entry Point

The build is invoked via the wrapper scripts:
- **Linux/macOS:** `./build.sh <Target>`
- **Windows:** `./build.cmd <Target>`

The CI pipeline calls `./build.cmd Test` (the default aggregate target).

> **Never call `build.ps1` directly** in CI steps or agent recommendations. Use `./build.cmd` or `./build.sh`. The wrapper scripts are the stable interface.

For local development, `dotnet build` and `dotnet test` work against individual projects. Use `./build.cmd Test` for full pipeline simulation.

### Target Graph

```
Test
├── UnitTests
│   └── Compile
│       └── Restore
└── E2ETests
    └── PlaywrightInstall
        └── Compile
            └── Restore
```

| Target | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `Restore` | Restores NuGet packages. On non-Windows: per-project loop (skips MAUI). On Windows: full solution. |
| `Compile` | Builds all buildable projects. Same OS branch as Restore. |
| `UnitTests` | Runs all `*.Tests` projects with `--no-build --no-restore`. |
| `PlaywrightInstall` | Locates the built `playwright.ps1` in the first `*.E2ETests` bin dir, runs `playwright install chromium --with-deps`. Required on Linux. |
| `E2ETests` | Runs all `*.E2ETests` projects with `--no-build --no-restore`. Depends on `PlaywrightInstall`, runs after `UnitTests`. |
| `Test` | Aggregate. Depends on `UnitTests` + `E2ETests`. This is what CI invokes. |
| `Clean` | Cleans build output. Runs before `Restore`. Not in the default CI chain. |

### Project Discovery

`Build.cs` discovers projects by convention: unit tests match `*.Tests`, E2E tests match `*.E2ETests`. Windows-only projects are listed in `NonWindowsExcludedProjects` in `Build.cs` (currently `LinqStudio.App.Maui` and `LinqStudio.AppHost`) and are skipped on non-Windows runners.

**Key rule:** When adding a new Windows-only project (MAUI, WinUI, etc.), add its name to `NonWindowsExcludedProjects` in `Build.cs`. This prevents workload errors on the ubuntu-latest runner without touching any YAML.

---

## GitHub Actions Workflows

### `build-and-test.yml` — Main CI (Auto-Generated)

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Trigger | `pull_request` (all PRs) |
| Runner | `ubuntu-latest` |
| Invoked via | `./build.cmd Test` |
| Caches | `.nuke/temp`, `~/.nuget/packages` — keyed on `global.json`, `*.csproj`, `Directory.Packages.props` |
| Token | `GITHUB_TOKEN` injected from `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` |

**Do not edit this file.** All changes go into `build/Build.cs`. Re-run Nuke generation to update the YAML.

### `build-maui-windows.yml` — MAUI Windows CI (Hand-Authored)

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Trigger | `pull_request` with path filter |
| Runner | `windows-latest` |
| Workload | `dotnet workload install maui-windows` (not `maui` — skips Android/iOS/Tizen) |
| Build command | `dotnet build src/LinqStudio.App.Maui/LinqStudio.App.Maui.csproj --configuration Release` |

**Path filter** — only runs when these paths change:
- `src/LinqStudio.App.Maui/**`
- `src/LinqStudio.Blazor/**`
- `src/LinqStudio.Core/**`
- `src/LinqStudio.Abstractions/**`

This is intentional cost control — Windows runners are ~2× more expensive than Linux.

**Safe to edit directly** — this file is not generated by Nuke.

---

## Global Build Settings(`Directory.Build.props`)

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
  <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
  <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
  <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**`TreatWarningsAsErrors=true` is global.** Any warning introduced — in any project including MAUI — will fail the CI build. This applies to both the ubuntu-latest and windows-latest runners.

---

## MAUI Exclusion Pattern

**Root cause:** `LinqStudio.App.Maui` targets `net10.0-windows10.0.19041.0`. MSBuild triggers MAUI workload validation at restore time, before any platform conditions are evaluated. On `ubuntu-latest`, this causes `NETSDK1147: maui-tizen workload required`.

**Solution in `Build.cs`:** On non-Windows, `Restore` and `Compile` iterate over `BuildableProjects` (MAUI excluded) instead of restoring/building the full solution.

**Do not use `dotnet restore LinqStudio.slnx`** directly on Linux — it will trigger MAUI workload errors. Always use the Nuke target (`./build.cmd Restore` or `./build.cmd Test`) which handles OS branching.

**`LinqStudio.AppHost`** is also excluded on non-Windows because it references the MAUI project via Aspire orchestration.

### Adding a New Windows-Only Project

1. Add the project name to `NonWindowsExcludedProjects` in `build/Build.cs`
2. Verify `BuildableProjects` excludes it on non-Windows
3. If it needs CI validation, add a path-filtered `windows-latest` job (hand-authored, like `build-maui-windows.yml`)
4. Do NOT install workloads on the ubuntu-latest runner just to support a Windows-only project

---

## MAUI Build Rules (Windows Runner)

- Use `dotnet workload install maui-windows` — NOT `maui`. The full `maui` workload includes Android, iOS, and Tizen, adding 6–8 minutes of install time.
- Do NOT split restore and build for MAUI targets unless you explicitly pass `--runtime win-x64` to restore. A bare `dotnet restore` without RID skips the Windows runtime pack, causing `NETSDK1112` on the subsequent `dotnet build --no-restore`.
- Single-step build (`dotnet build --configuration Release`) is the correct pattern — implicit restore handles the runtime pack correctly.
- The MAUI csproj uses `WindowsPackageType=None` (unpackaged Win32). No MSIX signing or packaging steps are needed in CI.

---

## Playwright (E2E Tests)

- The `PlaywrightInstall` Nuke target locates `playwright.ps1` in the E2E project's `bin/` output and runs it with `pwsh`.
- The install command is `playwright install chromium --with-deps` — Chromium only, not all browsers, to minimize install time.
- `--with-deps` is required on Linux (installs OS-level browser dependencies).
- E2E tests run via `DotNetTest` with `--no-build --no-restore`, so `PlaywrightInstall` (and therefore `Compile`) must run first.

---

## Build Commands Quick Reference

| Scenario | Command |
|---|---|
| Full CI pipeline (local simulation) | `./build.cmd Test` |
| Just unit tests via Nuke | `./build.cmd UnitTests` |
| Just E2E tests via Nuke | `./build.cmd E2ETests` |
| Build a single project | `dotnet build src/<Project>/<Project>.csproj` |
| Run unit tests for a project | `dotnet test src/<Project>/<Project>.Tests.csproj` |
| MAUI build (Windows only) | `dotnet build src/LinqStudio.App.Maui/LinqStudio.App.Maui.csproj --configuration Release` |

---

## Common CI Failure Patterns

| Error | Likely Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `NETSDK1147: maui-tizen workload required` | Restoring full solution on Linux | Add project to `NonWindowsExcludedProjects` in Build.cs |
| `NETSDK1112: runtime pack not available` | Split restore/build on MAUI without `--runtime` | Use single `dotnet build` (no `--no-restore`) |
| Warning treated as error | New warning introduced; `TreatWarningsAsErrors=true` is global | Fix the warning |
| Playwright test timeout | Browser not installed, or app not starting | Check `PlaywrightInstall` step; verify app starts on port |
| `actions/checkout@v6` not found | Auto-generated YAML references non-existent tag | Do not fix in YAML — it's a Nuke generator issue |

---

## Anti-Patterns

**Never do these:**

1. **Edit `build-and-test.yml` directly.** Auto-generated from `Build.cs` — changes will be overwritten.
2. **Run `dotnet workload install maui` (full).** Use `maui-windows`; the full workload adds Android/iOS/Tizen unnecessarily.
3. **Install workloads on ubuntu-latest for Windows-only projects.** Add the project to `NonWindowsExcludedProjects` in `Build.cs` instead.
4. **Split restore/build for MAUI without `--runtime`.** Use single-step `dotnet build`; splitting causes `NETSDK1112`.
5. **Call `build.ps1` directly.** Use `./build.cmd` (Windows) or `./build.sh` (Linux).
6. **Restore the full solution on Linux.** `dotnet restore LinqStudio.slnx` triggers MAUI workload errors; use `./build.cmd Restore` instead.
